title: Communicating Sequential Processes (CSP)
overview: "Do not communicate by sharing memory; instead, share memory by communicating." Channels act as the pipes through which data flows between concurrent tasks.
historical_context: Formalized by Tony Hoare in 1978 as a mathematical model for concurrent systems, CSP reacted against the complexity of lock-based concurrency.
mental_model: Think of discrete processes connected by unidirectional pipes (channels) that synchronize during communication.
key_innovations: Strict separation of concerns in concurrent logic.
tradeoffs: Potential for deadlocks if channel buffers are full or if processes are waiting indefinitely.
legacy: Directly implemented in languages like Go, Occam, and libraries for Clojure (core.async).
ai_assisted_discovery_missions: Compare the CSP model of Go with the Actor model of Erlang.
Explain the theoretical foundations of Tony Hoare’s 1978 paper.
Analyze how Clojure’s core.async brings CSP to a dynamic Lisp.
